Package de.uka.ilkd.key.logic

package de.uka.ilkd.key.logic

provides a representation for the term structure. The structure of a term is defined in JTerm. Subclasses of JTerm provide representations for special kinds of terms. However, these subclasses are supposed to be not directly accessed. Instead, JTerm provides methods for all the methods of the subclasses. Moreover, JTerms (or their subclasses) are supposed to be never constructed by constructors of their own, but by instances of TermFactory.

The function of JTerms (e.g., if they represent a conjunction of subterms, a quantified formula,...) is only determined by an Operator that is assigned to a JTerm when the JTerm is constructed.

JTerms are immutable.

Last modified: Wed Dec 4 15:11:14 MET 2002

    In contrast to the distinction of formulas and terms as made by most of the inductive definitions of the syntax of a logic, an instance of this class can stand for a term or a formula.
